text:
There used to be a library down the street from my house. It was always packed. People came here to lose themselves in stories, in worlds crafted by knowledgeable hands. But me? I was just an addict.

One particular story had haunted me. It was about a wolf who loved buttons. That's odd, you think? But to her eyes, they sparkled just like stars. Somehow, that tale had become her own little obsession. She spent nights chasing those buttons through the forest.

One evening, twilight slipping into dusk, I decided to talk to her about it. "Those buttons," I said. "You should stop."

She blinked. "Why?"

"The world of buttons?"

"Nah. The magic."

"I don't believe in magic."

She shrugged. "But I want to."

With that, I offered to help her find her buttons. We spent hours wandering through the forest, learning to see what mattered. Buttons everywhere—a mouse's hoard, birds nests. She was
